findings.title: Latency observation now depends on external metrics bootstrap and can panic in ingest paths
findings.why: The new `Observe` calls dereference package-level `prometheus.Observer` globals that stay nil until `pkg/metrics.InitMetrics()` runs. `ingestcli` itself does not perform that initialization, so any caller that uses this package without the global metrics bootstrap will crash instead of completing or returning the original write/ingest result.
findings.scope: pkg/ingestor/ingestcli/client.go:122-126 and pkg/ingestor/ingestcli/client.go:271-274, with initialization only in pkg/ingestor/ingestmetric/metric.go:30-49 via pkg/metrics/metrics.go:101
findings.risk_if_unchanged: Standalone or lightly wired consumers of `ingestcli` can hit a process panic on request completion, turning a non-critical observability addition into an availability failure. The package's own isolated tests are also exposed to this path because they import `ingestcli`/`ingestmetric` directly without importing `pkg/metrics`.
findings.evidence: `WriteAPIDuration` and `IngestAPIDuration` are declared as zero-value globals in `pkg/ingestor/ingestmetric/metric.go` and are only populated inside `InitIngestMetrics()`, which is called indirectly from `pkg/metrics.InitMetrics()` in `pkg/metrics/metrics.go:101`. The new defer blocks in `startChunkedHTTPRequest` and `Ingest` call `.Observe(...)` unconditionally, so when that bootstrap has not happened the interface value is nil and the request path panics. This looks reachable already in `pkg/ingestor/ingestcli/client_test.go`, which uses the package directly but does not import `pkg/metrics`.
findings.change_request: Please add self-contained initialization or a nil-safe guard before observing here; can we avoid relying on an external `pkg/metrics` import side effect for a request-path metric?
